2603.16091.title: CounterRefine: Answer-Conditioned Counterevidence Retrieval for Inference-Time Knowledge Repair in Factual Question Answering
2603.16091.paper_summary: In factual question answering, many errors are not failures of access but failures of commitment: the system retrieves relevant evidence, yet still settles on the wrong answer. We present CounterRefine, a lightweight inference-time repair layer for retrieval-grounded question answering. CounterRefine first produces a short answer from retrieved evidence, then gathers additional support and conflicting evidence with follow-up queries conditioned on that draft answer, and finally applies a restricted refinement step that outputs either KEEP or REVISE, with proposed revisions accepted only if they pass deterministic validation. In effect, CounterRefine turns retrieval into a mechanism for testing a provisional answer rather than merely collecting more context. On the full SimpleQA benchmark, CounterRefine improves a matched GPT-5 Baseline-RAG by 5.8 points and reaches a 73.1 percent correct rate, while exceeding the reported one-shot GPT-5.4 score by roughly 40 points. These findings suggest a simple but important direction for knowledgeable foundation models: beyond accessing evidence, they should also be able to use that evidence to reconsider and, when necessary, repair their own answers.
2603.16091.paper_summary_zh: 在事實性問題回答中，許多錯誤並不是訪問失敗，而是承諾失敗：系統檢索到相關證據，但仍然選擇了錯誤的答案。我們提出了CounterRefine，一個輕量級的推理時修復層，用於基於檢索的問題回答。CounterRefine首先從檢索到的證據中生成一個簡短的答案，然後根據該草擬答案進行後續查詢，收集額外的支持和矛盾證據，最後應用一個限制性的修正步驟，輸出KEEP或REVISE，只有在通過確定性驗證的情況下，才接受提出的修訂。實際上，CounterRefine將檢索轉變為測試臨時答案的機制，而不僅僅是收集更多的上下文。在完整的SimpleQA基準測試中，CounterRefine將匹配的GPT-5 Baseline-RAG提高了5.8分，達到了73.1%的正確率，同時超過了報告中的一次性GPT-5.4分數約40分。這些發現表明了一個簡單但重要的方向，對於知識豐富的基礎模型來說：除了訪問證據，它們還應能夠利用這些證據重新考慮並在必要時修正自己的答案。
2603.16091.author: Tianyi Huang et.al.
